question: What is the concept of cell-free peptide biosynthesis?
options: Only living cells can synthesize peptides
Using cell lysates containing the translational machinery to produce peptides without intact cells
A type of chemical synthesis only
Using dead cells that still grow
explanation: Cell-free systems (PURE extract, E. coli S30 lysate) provide all necessary components for transcription and translation, enabling rapid peptide production without maintaining cell viability.
